title: Garlicky Vietnamese Hind Shank Bun Thit Nuong
description: A garlicky and velvety Vietnamese dish featuring hind shank, perfect for a lazy Sunday, made with kombu-cold-extract and Whole30-friendly ingredients. This dish is a variation of the traditional bun thit nuong.

ingredients.name: fish sauce
ingredients.notes: make sure it is Whole30 compliant

instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot, combine the hind shank, kombu, garlic, fish sauce, coconut sugar, black pepper, and rice vinegar.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven to braise for 2 1/2 hours.
While the hind shank is braising, prepare the grilled pork paste according to the recipe or package instructions.
After 2 1/2 hours, remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the hind shank into bite-sized pieces and discard the bones.
In a large bowl, combine the shredded hind shank, grilled pork paste, pickled carrots, and chopped cilantro.
Toss everything together until well combined.
Serve the Garlicky Vietnamese Hind Shank Bun Thit Nuong over rice noodles or lettuce leaves.
